hero.headline: How much actually stays in your pocket?
hero.subtitle: Net income calculator for tax year 2026

results.headline: Results
results.emptyState: Enter your salary above to see the result

disclaimer: Results are approximate estimates for informational and educational purposes only. They do not constitute tax, financial, or legal advice and should not be used as the sole basis for any financial decision. Consult a qualified accountant for professional tax advice. Based on Law 5246/2025.

footer.taxYear: Calculation parameters verified as of March 2026 · Based on Law 5246/2025 · Not tax advice

compare.headline: Compare employment types
compare.subtitle: Same gross income — which arrangement puts more money in your pocket over a year?
compare.emptyState: Enter a monthly income to compare all three options
compare.whyDifferent: Why is the employee monthly paycheck lower?
compare.whyDifferentBody: Greek employees receive 14 salaries per year — 12 regular + Christmas bonus + Easter bonus + summer leave bonus. The annual total is higher, but each paycheck is 1/14 of that, not 1/12. The monthly equivalent rows above divide the annual net by 12 so all three options are on the same footing.
compare.defaults: Comparison uses: 0 children, age 35, 1st EFKA category, 4 years in business, no expenses.

whoWeAre.heading: 1. Who we are
whoWeAre.text: PocketMath.gr is a free educational and informational tool operated as a personal learning project based in Greece. Its purpose is to help people better understand gross pay, net pay, taxes, deductions, and common Greek work arrangements. For privacy questions or data-rights requests, contact us at tsatsarisgiorgos@gmail.com.

whatData.heading: 2. What personal data we process
whatData.intro: PocketMath is designed to minimise data processing and keep calculations on your device wherever possible.
whatData.calculations: Calculation data: All tax calculations are performed entirely within your browser (client-side). The salary, income, or other financial figures you enter are never transmitted to our servers, never stored by us, and are not accessible to us in any way.
whatData.serverLogs: Server and security logs: When you visit this website, our hosting provider automatically processes limited technical request data such as your IP address, requested page, timestamp, user-agent/browser details, and basic security or event logs for the purpose of delivering, securing, and troubleshooting the website.
whatData.hosting: Hosting provider: This website is hosted on GitHub Pages (served through the Fastly content delivery network). As part of delivering and securing the site, the hosting provider acts as a data processor and automatically records server-access logs that include your IP address and request metadata. This processing is carried out for delivery, performance, and security purposes under GitHub's own privacy terms.
whatData.language: Language preference: We store your language preference (Greek or English) in your browser's localStorage. This is a strictly necessary functional storage that allows the website to display content in your chosen language. This data remains on your device, is not transmitted to our servers, and can be deleted at any time by clearing your browser data.
whatData.theme: Theme preference: We store your chosen colour theme (dark or light) in your browser's localStorage. Like the language preference, this is strictly necessary functional storage that remains on your device and is never transmitted to our servers.
whatData.emailContact: Email contact: If you email us, we receive your email address, name or alias, message content, and any attachments or other information you choose to include. Please do not send special-category personal data, tax identification numbers, copies of IDs, bank details, or detailed payroll documents unless strictly necessary.
whatData.sponsorships: Voluntary sponsorships: If you choose to support the project through the external Revolut link, we may see limited transaction information made available by Revolut, such as your display name or username, payment reference, amount, currency, and the date of the payment. We do not collect or store your full card or bank details on this website.
whatData.noCookies: We do not use cookies or similar technologies for tracking, analytics, profiling, or advertising. We do not run third-party analytics, remarketing pixels, or ad-tech scripts. We do not create user accounts or user profiles.

legalBasis.heading: 3. Legal basis for processing
legalBasis.text: We process the limited personal data described above on the following legal bases under GDPR Article 6(1) and the applicable Greek ePrivacy rules:
legalBasis.serverLogs: Server and security logs: Legitimate interest (Art. 6(1)(f)) in operating, securing, monitoring, and troubleshooting the website.
legalBasis.localStorage: Language and theme preferences (localStorage): Strictly necessary for the service functionality as requested by you, exempt from consent under Art. 5(3) of the ePrivacy Directive (Greek Law 3471/2006, Art. 4(5)).
legalBasis.emailContact: Emails you send us: Legitimate interest (Art. 6(1)(f)) in replying to your message, handling legal or privacy requests, and maintaining the project.
legalBasis.sponsorships: Voluntary sponsorships via Revolut: Processing of limited transaction information visible to us is necessary to receive and administer the payment you choose to make (Art. 6(1)(b)) and, where needed, for legitimate interests in fraud prevention, record-keeping, and resolving disputes (Art. 6(1)(f)).

dataSharing.heading: 4. Data sharing and international transfers
dataSharing.text: We do not sell or rent your personal data. Limited technical log data may be processed by Vercel, our hosting provider, to host and secure the site. If you email us, Google/Gmail may process your message and related metadata as part of the email service. If you choose to sponsor the project, Revolut processes the payment under its own terms and privacy policy; we do not receive your full card or bank details through this website. These providers may process data outside Greece and, in some cases, outside the EEA/UK. Where applicable, we rely on the provider's contractual and statutory transfer safeguards for such transfers.

retention.heading: 5. Data retention
retention.serverLogs: Server and security logs: For the period determined by Vercel's operational and security retention schedule, and only for as long as reasonably necessary for hosting, security, and abuse prevention.
retention.localStorage: localStorage preferences (language, theme): Until you clear your browser data or change the setting.
retention.contactEmails: Contact emails: Usually until the matter is closed and for up to 24 months afterwards, unless a longer retention period is needed to document a legal or privacy request or defend against claims.
retention.sponsorships: Sponsorship records visible to us: For as long as reasonably necessary for bookkeeping, fraud prevention, dispute handling, and compliance with applicable tax or accounting obligations.

rights.heading: 6. Your rights
rights.intro: Under the GDPR and Greek Law 4624/2019, you have the right to:
rights.list: Access information about what personal data we process; request correction of inaccurate data; request deletion of your personal data; request limitation of processing; object to processing based on legitimate interest; lodge a complaint with the Hellenic Data Protection Authority (HDPA/APDPX).
rights.howTo: To exercise any of these rights, contact us at tsatsarisgiorgos@gmail.com. We will respond within 30 days.
rights.hdpa: Hellenic Data Protection Authority — Kifissias 1-3, 115 23 Athens, Greece — Tel: +30 210 6475600 — contact@dpa.gr — www.dpa.gr

children.heading: 7. Children's privacy
children.text: PocketMath is not directed at children. You must be at least 15 years old to use this website, in accordance with Greek Law 4624/2019 (Art. 21) implementing GDPR Article 8. We do not knowingly collect personal data from anyone under 15.

changes.heading: 8. Changes to this policy
changes.text: We may update this policy to reflect changes in our practices or applicable law. The "Last updated" date at the top indicates the most recent revision.

acceptance.heading: 1. Acceptance of terms
acceptance.text: By accessing or using PocketMath.gr, you agree to be bound by these Terms of Use. If you do not agree with any part of these terms, you must not use this website. You must be at least 15 years old to use this service.

nature.heading: 2. Nature of this service
nature.text: PocketMath.gr is a free educational and informational tool, operated as a personal learning project, that helps users estimate gross-to-net income, tax, social-security contributions, and comparable outcomes under Greek tax legislation. Its purpose is to help people better understand their payslips and stop thinking only in terms of desired monthly net income. This website is NOT a tax advisory service, accounting service, payroll outsourcing service, legal service, employment service, or financial advisory service. Use of the website does not create an accountant-client, adviser-client, fiduciary, employment, or other professional relationship.

noAdvice.heading: 3. No tax advice
noAdvice.intro: The calculations, results, figures, and information provided by this website:
list: Are approximate estimates provided for general informational and educational purposes only
Do NOT constitute tax advice, financial advice, legal advice, or any form of professional advice
Do NOT replace consultation with a qualified certified accountant or tax advisor
Should NOT be used as the sole basis for any financial decision, tax filing, tax payment, or legal action
May differ from your actual payslip, withholding, bonuses, benefits, social-security classification, or case-specific facts
Do NOT create any obligation for the operator to review your individual case or answer questions
May contain errors, inaccuracies, or omissions despite our reasonable efforts to ensure accuracy
noAdvice.responsibility: You are solely responsible for verifying all calculations with a qualified professional before making any financial or tax-related decisions.

accuracy.heading: 4. Legislative basis and accuracy
accuracy.text: Calculations are based on the provisions of Greek Law 5246/2025 and related tax and social security legislation, as understood and interpreted by the website operator at the time of implementation. The tax rates, brackets, social security contribution rates, tax credits, and other parameters reflected in this tool are accurate as of March 2026. Tax legislation may change, and these parameters may be amended, replaced, or reinterpreted by Greek authorities at any time. We endeavour to update this tool when legislative changes occur, but we do not guarantee that the tool reflects the current state of the law at any given time.
accuracy.asOf: The phrase "as of [date]" indicates the date on which the legislative parameters were last verified by the operator. It does not guarantee accuracy for any subsequent period, even if the underlying legislation has not changed.

liability.heading: 5. Limitation of liability
liability.intro: To the maximum extent permitted by Greek law:
list: The website is provided "as is" and "as available" without any warranty, express or implied, regarding accuracy, completeness, reliability, or fitness for any particular purpose

The operator shall not be liable for any direct, indirect, incidental, special, consequential, or punitive damages arising from or related to any errors in calculations, any reliance placed on the results, any financial decisions made based on information from this website, or any tax penalties resulting from use of this tool
The operator is not liable for downtime, hosting failures, email delivery issues, payment-provider issues, or third-party service interruptions outside the operator's reasonable control
This limitation applies regardless of the legal theory under which liability is asserted, including tort or contract

sponsorships.heading: 6. Voluntary sponsorships
sponsorships.text: The Revolut link in the footer allows voluntary sponsorships to support the project. Any sponsorship is optional, is not a purchase of digital content or professional services, and does not entitle you to premium features, priority support, a response, consulting, or any ongoing obligation. Any payment is processed entirely by Revolut under its own terms and privacy policy.

ip.heading: 7. Intellectual property
ip.text: All content, code, design, and calculations on this website are the property of the operator unless otherwise stated. Greek tax legislation referenced herein is public law and is not claimed as proprietary content.

externalLinks.heading: 8. External links
externalLinks.text: This website contains links to third-party websites and services, including Revolut for voluntary sponsorships. We are not responsible for the content, privacy practices, availability, security, or terms of those external services. Accessing them is at your own risk and subject to their own terms.

availability.heading: 9. Service availability
availability.text: PocketMath is provided on an 'as available' basis. We may modify, suspend, or discontinue any part of the service at any time without notice. We are not liable for any interruption or unavailability of the service.

law.heading: 10. Governing law and jurisdiction
law.text: These terms are governed by the laws of the Hellenic Republic. Any disputes shall be subject to the exclusive jurisdiction of the courts of Athens, Greece.

changes.heading: 11. Changes to these terms
changes.text: We reserve the right to modify these terms at any time. Continued use of the website after changes constitutes acceptance of the modified terms.
